decks.notes: The main idea behind this Selesnya Aggro deck is to quickly overwhelm your opponent with a combination of efficient and cheap creatures, such as Llanowar Elves and Skyclave Apparition, while disrupting their game plan with cards like Thalia, Guardian of Thraben. By using Collected Company to dig for more threats and building a wide board presence, this deck aims to put pressure on the opponent early on and finish the game before they have a chance to stabilize. With cards like Elspeth, Storm Slayer providing additional reach, this deck is all about aggression and tempo.

The main idea of this deck is to control the game by disrupting the opponent's strategy with cards like Thoughtseize and Fatal Push, while also building up a board presence with creatures like Badgermole Cub and Graveyard Trespasser. The deck aims to outvalue the opponent with powerful creatures like Sheoldred, the Apocalypse and close out the game with cards like Abrupt Decay. The inclusion of cards like Liliana of the Veil and Duress further adds to the control aspect of the deck, making it difficult for the opponent to execute their game plan effectively.

The deck focuses on utilizing spells and abilities to quickly bring out Arclight Phoenix from the graveyard, creating a constant source of aggression and pressure on the opponent. It emphasizes card draw and selection to find the necessary pieces for this strategy, with additional support from other aggressive creatures and removal spells for defense. Key cards like Artist's Talent and Consider enable the player to set up their graveyard efficiently, while Spirebluff Canal and Steam Vents provide the necessary mana base for the deck's fast-paced gameplay.

The deck focuses on fast and aggressive gameplay, utilizing cheap and efficient creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Soul-Scar Mage to apply pressure early on. Cards like Reckless Rage and Fiery Impulse help to control the board and remove opposing threats, while spells like Academic Dispute and Boomerang Basics provide extra card advantage and disruption. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a combination of creature damage and burn spells, utilizing UR colors to maximize tempo and aggression.

The deck focuses on assembling the combo of Greasefang, Okiba Boss and Parhelion II to create a powerful board state. It uses cards like Fleeting Spirit, Monument to Endurance, and Sheltered by Ghosts to protect and support the combo pieces. The deck also includes disruption spells like Thoughtseize and Fatal Push to control the game and ensure the combo can go off smoothly. Overall, the deck aims to efficiently put together its key pieces and overwhelm the opponent with a strong and resilient combo.

The deck focuses on utilizing Niv-Mizzet Reborn as a powerful control finisher, with a variety of multicolor cards to trigger his ability and generate card advantage. The deck also includes plenty of ramp and fixing to ensure you can cast your spells on time. The addition of Yorion, Sky Nomad as a companion gives you access to additional resources and flexibility in your gameplay. Overall, the deck aims to control the game with a mix of removal, card draw, and powerful threats to outlast your opponent and secure victory.

This deck focuses on controlling the game through a combination of removal spells, versatile creatures, and powerful spells like Niv-Mizzet Reborn and Bring to Light. The deck includes a wide range of lands to ensure mana fixing and consistency, along with a few key companions like Yorion, Sky Nomad. By utilizing cards like Leyline Binding, Vanishing Verse, and Abrupt Decay, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan while establishing its own board presence. Through card advantage and efficient removal, the deck seeks to outvalue opponents and secure victory.

The main concept of this UR Aggro deck is to quickly build a board presence with low-cost creatures like Soul-Scar Mage, Monastery Swiftspear, and Cori-Steel Cutter, while utilizing burn spells like Fiery Impulse and Reckless Rage to control the board and clear the way for attacks. The deck also includes cards like Heroes' Hangout and Riverpyre Verge to provide additional card draw and mana fixing. By combining efficient creatures with burn spells and card advantage, this deck aims to apply pressure early and maintain that pressure throughout the game.

This deck focuses on utilizing a combination of cards that allow for creative and unpredictable plays. By using cards like Indomitable Creativity, Fable of the Mirror-Breaker, and Overlord of the Mistmoors, the player can consistently generate powerful combos and disrupt the opponent's game plan. The deck also includes cards like Lightning Helix for removal and protection. The key strategy is to surprise the opponent with unexpected plays and overwhelming board presence, while utilizing the unique abilities of the cards to gain an advantage in the match.

The main concept of this deck is to overwhelm your opponent with fast and aggressive creatures, while also utilizing burn spells to remove blockers or finish off your opponent. The deck focuses on playing low-cost creatures like Soul-Scar Mage and Monastery Swiftspear, along with spells like Fiery Impulse and Reckless Rage to control the board and push damage through. By putting pressure on your opponent early and using efficient removal, you aim to close out the game quickly before your opponent can stabilize.

The deck focuses on utilizing Transmogrify as a key card to cheat big creatures into play, such as Atraxa, Grand Unifier and Reckoner Bankbuster. It also includes various removal spells and hand disruption cards to control the game while setting up the combo. The deck aims to quickly assemble the combo pieces and win the game through overwhelming creature presence and disruption of the opponent's game plan. It also includes some utility lands and additional spell options for added versatility and flexibility in different matchups.

This UR Aggro deck focuses on quickly establishing a board presence and dealing damage to the opponent. The deck includes efficient creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Soul-Scar Mage, along with burn spells like Burst Lightning and Reckless Rage to control the board and finish off the opponent. The deck also includes card draw and tempo plays with cards like Academic Dispute and Brazen Borrower. With a mix of creatures, burn spells, and card draw, this deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with its aggressive strategy.

The main concept of this deck is to control the game by using a combination of removal spells, hand disruption, and powerful creatures. The deck focuses on using cards like Thoughtseize and Fatal Push to disrupt the opponent's game plan, while also utilizing creatures like Gifted Aetherborn and Sheoldred, the Apocalypse to apply pressure and generate advantage. The deck aims to grind out the opponent by constantly removing threats and out-valuing them with efficient creatures and spells. Additionally, the deck utilizes lands like Mutavault and Castle Locthwain to provide additional utility and card advantage.

The deck focuses on combining various cards to create powerful synergies and overwhelming your opponent with a combination of control elements and aggressive creatures. The key strategy involves using cards like Fatal Push and Thoughtseize to disrupt your opponent's game plan while deploying creatures like Gran-Gran and Rona, Herald of Invasion to apply pressure on the board. Additionally, utilizing cards like Tinybones Joins Up and The Meathook Massacre can help to generate card advantage and deal with opposing threats effectively.

The deck focuses on using combo pieces like Greasefang and Parhelion II to create a powerful board presence and overwhelm the opponent. It includes support cards like Fatal Push and Guardian of New Benalia to control the board and protect the combo pieces. The deck also includes mana fixing lands like Concealed Courtyard and Godless Shrine to ensure the player can consistently cast their powerful spells. Overall, the strategy revolves around assembling the combo pieces quickly and efficiently to outpace the opponent and secure a victory.

The main idea of this Pioneer 1 vs 1 Aggro deck is to build up devotion to green and overwhelm the opponent with powerful creatures and efficient spells. By utilizing cards like Tyvar, the Pummeler and Wildborn Preserver alongside ramp creatures like Llanowar Elves and Elvish Mystic, the deck aims to flood the board with threats early on. Cards like Collected Company and Tribute to the World Tree provide additional card advantage and synergy with the green devotion strategy. Overall, the deck focuses on high tempo plays and aggressive tactics to quickly close out the game.
